page.title: Marbella International Film Festival: Premiere-Day Yacht Charter Playbook

data.meta_description: Watch the Marbella Film Festival premiere from a private yacht in Puerto Banús. From €749/2h with skipper, drinks, snacks & VAT. Book your boat now.
data.summary: A practical playbook for experiencing the Marbella International Film Festival premiere from a private yacht charter in Puerto Banús, with boat options from €749/2h.
key_facts: Marbella Film Festival premiere takes place in Puerto Banús, a 5-minute walk from our fleet.
Private yacht charters start at €749/2h on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39.
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance & 21% VAT.
Mangusta 80 (24m) offers a VIP deck for up to 12 guests from €4,719/4h (jet ski included).
Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús Marina, right at the festival hub.
Book via WhatsApp for last-minute availability on premiere day.
Sunset cruises (7–9pm) align perfectly with the film schedule.

faq.q: Can I watch the Marbella Film Festival premiere from a yacht?
faq.a: Yes. Our yachts moor in Puerto Banús Marina, where the premiere red-carpet events happen. You can watch the arrivals from the deck of your private charter, then cruise along the coast after the screening.

faq.q: Which yacht is best for the film festival premiere?
faq.a: For a large group, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax, from €4,719/4h) gives you a flybridge with panoramic views. For an intimate couple’s evening, the Mariah SX21 speedboat (6.4m, 5 pax) is a more affordable option.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for the Marbella Film Festival?
faq.a: You can book directly via WhatsApp or through our website. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance for premiere day. All charters include a skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, bottled water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs.

faq.q: Can I celebrate after the film on the yacht?
faq.a: Absolutely. You can book a 4-hour evening charter (e.g., 9pm–1am) to cruise the Marbella coastline after the premiere. Our boats are equipped with sound systems and ample deck space for a private party.

faq.q: What time does the film festival premiere start?
faq.a: The red carpet typically begins around 6pm, with screenings starting at 8pm. A sunset cruise from 7pm to 9pm lets you watch the arrivals, then head out to sea for the golden hour.

faq.q: Is there parking near Puerto Banús for the festival?
faq.a: Parking is limited during festival days. We recommend arriving by taxi or using the public car park at the marina. Alternatively, book a yacht with a skipper and avoid parking altogether.

faq.q: Can I charter a yacht for a private screening?
faq.a: Our yachts are not equipped for film projection, but you can anchor near the marina and watch the festival buzz from the water. For a private screening, we recommend booking a larger boat like the Ferretti 94 (29m) with a spacious saloon.

data.body_html:
The Marbella International Film Festival brings Hollywood glamour to the Costa del Sol every year, and there’s no better way to experience the premiere day than from the deck of a private yacht. Whether you’re spotting celebrities on the red carpet in Puerto Banús or cruising the Mediterranean after the credits roll, a charter boat gives you front-row access with zero crowds. Here’s your playbook for a seamless premiere-day yacht charter.

Why charter a yacht for the Marbella Film Festival premiere?

Puerto Banús is the epicentre of the festival’s red-carpet events. Our fleet departs from the same marina, meaning you can step off your boat and onto the carpet in under five minutes. A private yacht offers three advantages: guaranteed parking (the boat), a VIP vantage point (watch arrivals from the flybridge), and a post-screening party spot away from the packed restaurants. With 27°C average October temperatures, the evening air is perfect for alfresco dining on deck.

Our fleet for festival-goers

We operate 18 boats from Puerto Banús, ranging from a 6.4m speedboat to a 29m superyacht. For premiere day, we recommend these options:

BoatLengthMax guestsPrice fromBest for
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749/2hCouples or small groups
Azimut 3912.5m11€749/2hFamily or friends
Mangusta 8024m12€4,719/4hVIP red-carpet views
Ferretti 9429m12On requestLuxury entertaining

All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT. No hidden extras.

Premiere-day timeline

Here’s a sample schedule for a perfect evening:

  • 5:30pm – Board your yacht at Puerto Banús. Meet your skipper and enjoy welcome cava.
  • 6:00pm – Cruise out to the marina entrance for a view of the red-carpet arrivals.
  • 7:00pm – Anchor near the Golden Mile for sunset photos with La Concha in the background.
  • 8:00pm – Return to the marina for the screening, or stay onboard for a private dinner cruise.
  • 10:00pm – After the film, head to Cala del Faro for a night swim under the stars.

What’s included in every charter

No surprises. Every booking comes with:

  • Licensed English-speaking skipper
  • Fuel for the entire trip
  • Unlimited water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (olives, crisps, nuts)
  • Full insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%) – included in the price

You can add catering, a photographer, or extra hours via WhatsApp.

How to book for premiere day

Bookings are taken via WhatsApp or our website. We recommend reserving at least 48 hours in advance, especially for the Mangusta 80 which is popular for VIP groups. For last-minute availability, message us directly – we often have boats free on the day. Check our full fleet here and choose your vessel.

Where to anchor near the festival

Puerto Banús is the hub, but for quieter moments, these spots are within 15 minutes of the marina:

  • Cala Cortés – A secluded cove with turquoise water, ideal for a pre-film swim.
  • Río Verde – Calm waters near the mouth of the river, perfect for sunset.
  • Estepona coast – A 20-minute cruise west offers a more relaxed vibe away from the festival buzz.

Frequently asked questions

Can I watch the Marbella Film Festival premiere from a yacht?

Yes. Our yachts moor in Puerto Banús Marina, where the premiere red-carpet events happen. You can watch the arrivals from the deck of your private charter, then cruise along the coast after the screening.

Which yacht is best for the film festival premiere?

For a large group, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax, from €4,719/4h) gives you a flybridge with panoramic views. For an intimate couple’s evening, the Mariah SX21 speedboat (6.4m, 5 pax) is a more affordable option.

How do I book a yacht for the Marbella Film Festival?

You can book directly via WhatsApp or through our website. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance for premiere day. All charters include a skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and VAT.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, bottled water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs.

Can I celebrate after the film on the yacht?

Absolutely. You can book a 4-hour evening charter (e.g., 9pm–1am) to cruise the Marbella coastline after the premiere. Our boats are equipped with sound systems and ample deck space for a private party.

What time does the film festival premiere start?

The red carpet typically begins around 6pm, with screenings starting at 8pm. A sunset cruise from 7pm to 9pm lets you watch the arrivals, then head out to sea for the golden hour.

Is there parking near Puerto Banús for the festival?

Parking is limited during festival days. We recommend arriving by taxi or using the public car park at the marina. Alternatively, book a yacht with a skipper and avoid parking altogether.

Can I charter a yacht for a private screening?

Our yachts are not equipped for film projection, but you can anchor near the marina and watch the festival buzz from the water. For a private screening, we recommend booking a larger boat like the Ferretti 94 (29m) with a spacious saloon.
